content: Go's text/template performs NO auto-escaping (unlike html/template) — it is designed for trusted templates, not untrusted data. Passing CRD-sourced strings (hostnames, attribute IDs, entityIDs) straight into an XML template can break XML well-formedness or inject unintended elements/attributes if a value contains &, <, >, " or the literal -- inside a comment (matches spike fix K). Must run values through xml.EscapeText or a dedicated template func before interpolation, or build the XML via encoding/xml struct marshaling instead of text/template.
